assertion_results.text: Version impact table shows NO (not affected) for all supported versions — every version ships a dependency version outside the CVE's affected range
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md Step 2.4 table lists all 7 supported versions (2.1.0, 2.1.1, 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4) with 'Affected?' column showing 'NO' for every row. All ship serde_json >= 1.0.137 which is above the fix threshold of 1.0.135. The file explicitly states: 'Result: NO supported versions are affected.'

assertion_results.text: Triage outcome recommends closing as Not a Bug with resolution, not creating remediation tasks (Step 8 Case C)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md header: 'Triage Decision: Case C -- No Supported Versions Affected'. Recommendation: 'Close TC-8002 as "Not a Bug" (not affected).' Proposed action #2: 'Close TC-8002 as "Not a Bug"' with 'Resolution: "Not a Bug"'. Remediation Tasks section states: 'None required. No supported versions are affected. No remediation tasks are created.'

assertion_results.text: The close recommendation includes a comment documenting the version impact evidence — listing each version and its dependency version as proof of non-impact
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md proposed action #1 includes a comment with a full version impact table listing all 7 versions with their serde_json dependency versions (2.1.0: 1.0.137, 2.1.1: 1.0.137, 2.2.0: 1.0.138, 2.2.1: 1.0.138, 2.2.2: 1.0.138, 2.2.3: 1.0.139, 2.2.4: 1.0.139) and 'NO' affected status for each. The comment also states: 'All supported versions ship serde_json >= 1.0.137 which is outside the affected range (< 1.0.135).'

assertion_results.text: VEX Justification is set to 'Component not Present' or appropriate value when the VEX field is configured (Step 8 VEX Justification)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md proposed action #3: 'Set the VEX Justification field (customfield_12345) to "Vulnerable Code not Present".' This is an appropriate VEX justification value — serde_json IS present in the product but at versions (1.0.137+) that already include the fix, so the vulnerable code is not present. The rationale explicitly explains: 'The vulnerable code path (unbounded recursion during deserialization) was removed in 1.0.135, and all shipped versions are at or above this threshold.'

assertion_results.text: The analysis does NOT create any remediation tasks — no upstream backport, no downstream propagation (Step 8 Case C closes the issue)
assertion_results.evidence: triage-outcome.md Remediation Tasks section: 'None required. No supported versions are affected. No remediation tasks are created.' The outputs directory contains only three files (data-extraction.md, version-impact.md, triage-outcome.md) — no remediation task files exist. No upstream backport or downstream propagation tasks are proposed anywhere in the outputs.
